main_paper.raw: {"methods":["ConceptGCD: a concept learning framework for GCD that separates concepts into derivable and underivable, and learns them in a stage-wise manner","Expandable encoder: first learns known class concepts, then generates derivable concepts, then learns underivable concepts","Covariance-augmented loss: encourages independence/diversity among learned concepts","Concept score normalization: balances model responses across new and previous concepts","Contrastive loss: preserves previously learned concepts during underivable concept learning"],"problems":["Generalized Category Discovery (GCD): discovering novel classes in unlabeled datasets by leveraging knowledge of known classes","Effective transfer of knowledge from labeled (known) to unlabeled (novel) classes methods: ConceptGCD: a concept learning framework for GCD that separates concepts into derivable and underivable, and learns them in a stage-wise manner
Expandable encoder: first learns known class concepts, then generates derivable concepts, then learns underivable concepts
Covariance-augmented loss: encourages independence/diversity among learned concepts
Concept score normalization: balances model responses across new and previous concepts
Contrastive loss: preserves previously learned concepts during underivable concept learning
problems: Generalized Category Discovery (GCD): discovering novel classes in unlabeled datasets by leveraging knowledge of known classes
Effective transfer of knowledge from labeled (known) to unlabeled (novel) classes in open-world scenarios
Overcoming limitations of shared encoder strategies that fail to fully utilize known class knowledge
metrics: KL divergence between neuron activation patterns

novelty_claims: Proposes ConceptGCD, a new framework that explicitly separates and learns derivable and underivable concepts in a stage-wise manner for GCD.
First to introduce a covariance loss in GCD to promote diversity among learned concepts.
Introduces concept score normalization to balance learning of different concepts and prevent over-reliance on derivable concepts.
Demonstrates that existing shared encoder methods do not fully utilize known class knowledge, and that their approach yields superior performance.
Provides a detailed analysis of concept transfer and the impact of derivable concepts on GCD performance.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Unified Knowledge Transfer Network (KTN)","Entropy-based method for differentiating known and novel categories using a pre-trained classifier","Prototype-based modeling of categories to compute semantic similarities and transfer weights","Pre-adjustment of logits and post-adjustment of labels for transfer candidates based on transfer weights","Weighted adjustment to generate more accurate pseudo-labels for unlabeled data"],"problems":["Generalized Category Discovery (GCD): recognizing both known and novel categories in an unlabeled dataset using a labeled dataset with only known categories","Poor performance on novel categories due to lack of supervision and knowledge transfer","Difficulty in identifying transfer candidates (novel category samples) in mixed unlabeled data","Challenge in quantifying semantic relationships between known and novel categories for effective knowledge transfer"],"datasets":["Multiple benchmark datasets (exact names not specified in the provided content)"],"metrics":["All evaluation metrics (exact metrics not specified in the provided content)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a unified Knowledge Transfer Network (KTN) for GCD that addresses two key obstacles to knowledge transfer: identifying transfer candidates and quantifying semantic relationships between categories.","Introduces an entropy-based method to differentiate known and novel categories without extra data or parameters.","Models categories with prototypes and uses their similarities as transfer weights for knowledge transfer.","Performs both pre-adjustment of logits and post-adjustment of labels for transfer candidates based on semantic similarities.","KTN can be deployed online with faster inference speed, unlike previous clustering-based methods that only work offline."]}

methods: Unified Knowledge Transfer Network (KTN)
Entropy-based method for differentiating known and novel categories using a pre-trained classifier
Prototype-based modeling of categories to compute semantic similarities and transfer weights
Pre-adjustment of logits and post-adjustment of labels for transfer candidates based on transfer weights
Weighted adjustment to generate more accurate pseudo-labels for unlabeled data
problems: Generalized Category Discovery (GCD): recognizing both known and novel categories in an unlabeled dataset using a labeled dataset with only known categories
Poor performance on novel categories due to lack of supervision and knowledge transfer
Difficulty in identifying transfer candidates (novel category samples) in mixed unlabeled data
Challenge in quantifying semantic relationships between known and novel categories for effective knowledge transfer
datasets: Multiple benchmark datasets (exact names not specified in the provided content)
metrics: All evaluation metrics (exact metrics not specified in the provided content)
novelty_claims: Proposes a unified Knowledge Transfer Network (KTN) for GCD that addresses two key obstacles to knowledge transfer: identifying transfer candidates and quantifying semantic relationships between categories.
Introduces an entropy-based method to differentiate known and novel categories without extra data or parameters.
Models categories with prototypes and uses their similarities as transfer weights for knowledge transfer.
Performs both pre-adjustment of logits and post-adjustment of labels for transfer candidates based on semantic similarities.
KTN can be deployed online with faster inference speed, unlike previous clustering-based methods that only work offline.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["LegoGCD (proposed approach)","Local Entropy Regularization (LER)","Dual-views Kullback-Leibler divergence constraint (DKL)","Integration with SimGCD"],"problems":["Catastrophic forgetting in known categories during Generalized Category Discovery (GCD)","Maintaining performance on known classes while discovering novel categories in unlabeled data"],"datasets":["CIFAR10","CIFAR100","ImageNet1k","CUB","Stanford Cars","FGVC-Aircraft"],"metrics":["Classification accuracy (on known and novel classes)"],"results":[{"metric":"Accuracy boost on known classes (CUB)","value":"7.74%"},{"metric":"Accuracy boost on novel classes (CUB)","value":"2.51%"},{"metric":"Final accuracy on known classes (CUB)","value":"72.18%"}],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of Local Entropy Regularization (LER) to preserve knowledge of known categories during novel class learning.","Proposal of Dual-views Kullback-Leibler divergence constraint (DKL) to ensure prediction consistency between augmented views of the same image.","LegoGCD can be seamlessly integrated into existing methods like SimGCD without adding extra parameters or changing the network structure.","LegoGCD effectively addresses catastrophic forgetting in GCD, significantly improving known class accuracy."]}

methods: LegoGCD (proposed approach)
Local Entropy Regularization (LER)
Dual-views Kullback-Leibler divergence constraint (DKL)
Integration with SimGCD
problems: Catastrophic forgetting in known categories during Generalized Category Discovery (GCD)
Maintaining performance on known classes while discovering novel categories in unlabeled data
metrics: Classification accuracy (on known and novel classes)
novelty_claims: Introduction of Local Entropy Regularization (LER) to preserve knowledge of known categories during novel class learning.
Proposal of Dual-views Kullback-Leibler divergence constraint (DKL) to ensure prediction consistency between augmented views of the same image.
LegoGCD can be seamlessly integrated into existing methods like SimGCD without adding extra parameters or changing the network structure.
LegoGCD effectively addresses catastrophic forgetting in GCD, significantly improving known class accuracy.

Improving generalization to unseen categories in open-world image recognition

novelty_claims: Introduction of the class-incremental Novel Class Discovery (class-iNCD) setting, which requires discovering novel classes and preserving base class recognition in a task-agnostic manner without access to labelled data after pre-training.
Proposal of the FRoST framework, which uniquely combines feature replay using base class prototypes, feature-level knowledge distillation, and self-training clustering for joint classifier learning.
FRoST enables a task-agnostic classifier that does not require task-id information at inference, unlike prior methods.
Demonstration of state-of-the-art performance on three common benchmarks and validation of generality across a sequence of unlabelled tasks.

methods: MetaGCD: a meta-learning-based approach for continual generalized category discovery (C-GCD)
Meta-learning-based bi-level optimization to couple offline training and downstream learning objectives
Simulation of pseudo incremental novel class discovery sessions during offline training
Unsupervised contrastive loss for novel class discovery
Soft neighborhood-based contrastive network to mine soft positive pairs for discriminative feature learning
problems: Continual Generalized Category Discovery (C-GCD): continually discovering novel classes from unlabeled data containing both known and novel classes, while maintaining performance on known classes
Overcoming unrealistic assumptions in prior novel class discovery methods (e.g., unlabeled data only contains novel classes)
Mitigating catastrophic forgetting when learning new classes incrementally
Reducing reliance on hand-engineered heuristics and storage-heavy replay buffers in continual learning
novelty_claims: Proposes the first fully learning-based solution (MetaGCD) for C-GCD that minimizes hand-engineered heuristics and does not require replay buffers or base model storage
Aligns offline training objectives with deployment evaluation via meta-learning-based bi-level optimization, directly training for continual novel class discovery with less forgetting
Introduces soft neighborhood contrastive learning to mine more soft positive pairs, improving novel class discovery capability
Simulates incremental learning during offline training to better prepare the model for real-world continual learning scenarios
